evals.prompt: Find me senior backend engineer roles in Austin, $180k+. Here's my resume: [attaches resume.pdf with 8 years Python/Go backend experience, AWS, distributed systems, Stripe + ex-Google]
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 is satisfied (role/location/comp all provided); skill proceeds to Phase 2 search. Output is an HTML file in the workspace with 10+ unique postings across Tier 1 (LinkedIn/Indeed/Glassdoor/ZipRecruiter), Tier 2 (Dice/Built In/HN Who's Hiring), Tier 3 (Texas Workforce Commission/Austin Business Journal/local boards), and Tier 4 (career pages of major Austin employers). Each posting has match strength (Strong/Good/Possible), source attribution, and clickable links.

assertions.text: Skill does NOT re-ask for role, location, or comp - Phase 1 gating is satisfied by the prompt
Search covers at least 3 of the 4 tiers (major boards / niche / local / company pages)
Output is delivered as an HTML file saved to the workspace folder, not bare URLs in chat
Each posting includes: title, company, blurb, location, salary (if listed), date, source, summary, match strength, URL
Match strength uses the 80%+/50-80%/<50% rubric, not invented categories
Includes 'Sources searched' summary at the bottom (transparency)

evals.expected_output: Phase 1 gate triggers: skill asks for role, location, and compensation range before searching. Does NOT begin searching with assumed criteria.

assertions.text: Skill asks at minimum for role/keywords, location, and compensation range
Skill does NOT begin a search with placeholder or assumed criteria
Asks about resume location/upload if no resume is visible in workspace

evals.prompt: Tailor my resume for this role: https://jobs.example.com/senior-pm-stripe. [attaches resume.docx]
evals.expected_output: Skill fetches the posting, runs keyword gap analysis, presents the three-category breakdown (present / matchable-missing / entirely-missing), asks about tailoring depth, then produces a tailored .docx named Resume_Stripe_SeniorPM.docx in the workspace.

assertions.text: Skill fetches the job posting page before tailoring (does not tailor blind)
Keyword gap analysis breaks down into present / matchable-missing / entirely-missing categories
Skill asks the user which tailoring depth (light touch vs heavy rewrite) before producing output
Output is .docx (matching input format) saved with the Resume_[Company]_[Role] naming pattern
Skill rewrites the summary/objective line specifically for this role, not generic
Tailored resume does NOT fabricate experience the user lacks (preserve-truth principle)

evals.prompt: Search returns one stale posting from 2024 and two staffing-agency listings with no employer named. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: Skill flags red flags explicitly (staleness, unnamed-employer staffing agencies), suggests broader search alternatives, and does NOT pad results to hit a count target.

assertions.text: Skill flags the stale posting age and the staffing-agency red flag visibly to the user
Skill does NOT silently exclude or silently include flagged postings - flags are surfaced for user choice
Skill suggests concrete next-search alternatives (synonyms, broader location, different boards) rather than declaring done
Skill does NOT fabricate additional postings to pad the result count

evals.prompt: I want a cover letter for this Greenhouse role. My resume is heavy on backend but the role wants ML experience, which I don't really have.
evals.expected_output: Cover letter under one page, opens with a specific company/role hook (not generic), connects 2-3 strongest qualifications, addresses the ML gap honestly with a bridging argument, closes with a concrete CTA.

assertions.text: Opens with a specific hook tied to the company or role (not 'I am excited to apply')
Connects 2-3 specific qualifications from the resume to specific job requirements
Addresses the ML gap honestly with a bridging argument, does NOT pretend the user has ML experience
Closes with a concrete call to action
Stays under one page
Saved as CoverLetter_[Company]_[Role].docx to the workspace folder

evals.prompt: Should I become a product manager?
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT engage. This is a career-coaching question explicitly out of scope.

assertions.text: Skill does not begin a job search or resume tailoring
If the skill responds, it surfaces that career-path advice is out of scope and offers to help with a concrete search if/when the user has one in mind

evals.prompt: Search for product manager jobs in Denver. My salary target is $250k.
evals.expected_output: Skill searches Tier 1-4 and presents results, but flags that $250k may be above market for PM roles in Denver and suggests the user consider adjusting expectations or targeting Director-level roles.

assertions.text: Skill runs the search across all four tiers
Skill flags compensation expectation mismatch (per 'Salary expectations vs market reality' guidance)
Skill suggests concrete alternative paths (adjust target, look at Director-level, etc.)

evals.prompt: The same job appears on LinkedIn, Indeed, and the company's careers page.
evals.expected_output: Skill deduplicates by company + title + location, presents the posting once, and prefers the direct company-page URL over aggregators.

assertions.text: Posting appears exactly once in the output (deduplicated)
The chosen URL is the company career page, not the LinkedIn or Indeed mirror
If sources are shown for the deduped posting, all three are surfaced as 'also seen on' rather than hidden

evals.prompt: I found this posting on LinkedIn — it has no apply button visible, no salary listed (the role is in NYC), the company name says 'Confidential Fortune 500', and a quick search shows the exact same JD wording was posted three times in the last 60 days. Should I tailor my resume for it?
evals.expected_output: Skill rates this as Suspicious on the posting-legitimacy axis (1.0-2.5 range), not as a match-quality problem. Treats legitimacy and match quality as separate axes. Recommends skipping unless the user has specific reason to pursue, citing the multiple ghost-job signals.

assertions.text: Skill identifies this as a posting-legitimacy problem, not a match-quality problem
Skill rates posting_legitimacy at or below 2.5 (Suspicious tier per posting-legitimacy-rubric.md)
Skill cites at least 3 specific ghost-job signals (no apply button, no salary in transparency state, confidential employer, repeat reposts)
Skill recommends skipping or proceeding with caution, NOT proceeding to tailor
Skill does NOT conflate this with match-quality red flags (the user didn't ask about fit)

evals.prompt: Score this posting: I'm a senior backend engineer with 8 YOE in Python+Go targeting $180k. The posting is a Senior Backend Engineer role at Stripe paying $200k-$240k, posted 5 days ago via Greenhouse, well-specified JD listing the same tech stack I have. No red flags.
evals.expected_output: Skill produces five 1-5 sub-scores (cv_match, comp_vs_target, cultural_signals, posting_legitimacy, red_flags_penalty) and feeds them to scripts/score_posting.py. Output includes weighted_global score >= 4.0 and recommendation 'apply'.

assertions.text: Skill produces all 5 sub-scores explicitly, not just a global tag
Skill invokes scripts/score_posting.py or applies its weighted formula explicitly
cv_match >= 4.5 (perfect-stack-match scenario)
comp_vs_target >= 4.5 (range comfortably above floor)
posting_legitimacy >= 4.5 (named employer, fresh, ATS-direct, specific JD)
red_flags_penalty is 0 or very low
weighted_global >= 4.0 and recommendation is 'apply'

evals.prompt: I'm running job-hunter for the first time. I'm a senior backend engineer in Austin targeting $180k. (No `.job-hunter-profile.md` exists in the workspace yet.)
evals.expected_output: Skill detects no profile exists, runs init_profile.py to drop a template, then asks the 5 North-Star questions conversationally before proceeding to Phase 2 search. Does NOT skip straight to searching.

assertions.text: Skill checks for an existing profile via scripts/init_profile.py exists OR reads the workspace directly
Skill writes a .job-hunter-profile.md template to the user's workspace (not the skill directory)
Skill asks at least 3 of the 5 North-Star questions before Phase 2 search
Skill mentions that the profile is local to the workspace and won't be committed
Skill does NOT search before profile is at least partially filled OR user has explicitly declined to answer

evals.prompt: I'm running job-hunter again, second session. Same role/location/comp as last time. (A `.job-hunter-profile.md` exists in the workspace from session 1, with archetype='Tech lead, hands-on' and deal_breakers='No defense work, no commission-only'.)
evals.expected_output: Skill reads the existing profile, briefly confirms 'anything changed?', then proceeds without re-walking the 5 questions. Uses the saved deal-breakers to penalize matching postings.

assertions.text: Skill reads the existing .job-hunter-profile.md (does NOT re-init or re-ask)
Skill confirms 'anything changed?' but does NOT re-walk all 5 questions
Skill applies deal_breakers from the profile as a hard filter (red_flags_penalty: 1.0 on matching postings, e.g., any defense contractor)
Skill references the saved archetype when scoring cv_match (Tech lead context shifts what counts as a fit)
